### Post by slick666 on 2009-03-19
I'm not sure where to start looking to solve this problem. I can't seem to figure out what has changed but to the best of my knowledge. When I get on my network I can't resolve any web addresses. I added my router (192.168.0.1) as a nameserver and things seem to work fine. If I reconnect I have to edit my resolv.conf again.

Here's my resolv.conf file
> 
# Generated by NetworkManager
domain twcny.rr.com
search twcny.rr.com

nameserver 192.168.0.1


Any help appreciated.

---

### Post by Iowan on 2009-03-20
If you're getting DHCP address, look in */etc/dhcp3/dhclient.conf*.  There is a "prepend" line that can be changed to put your preferred nameserver at the top of the list.

---

### Post by slick666 on 2009-03-20
I don't know if it's something that is wrong with Ubuntu now. I brought a macbook on the network as well and it had the same problems and same solution.
